title: TinyMCE Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability using media plugin `data-mce-object` injection
description:
### Impact
Stored XSS vulnerability in the media plugin. Attackers can inject malicious scripts via crafted `data-mce-*` attributes, which are executed when content is rendered. Impacts users of TinyMCE with the media plugin enabled.

### Patches
This vulnerability has been patched in TinyMCE 8.5.1, TinyMCE 7.9.3 and TinyMCE 5.11.1 LTS by ensuring that, when using the media plugin, any content with `data-mce-object` and `data-mce-p-*` attributes are properly sanitized.

### Workarounds
No official workaround available.

### Fix
To avoid this vulnerability:

- Upgrade to TinyMCE 8.5.1 or higher.
- Upgrade to TinyMCE 7.9.3 or higher.
- Upgrade to TinyMCE 5.11.1 LTS or higher for TinyMCE 5.x (only available as part of commercial [long-term support](https://www.tiny.cloud/long-term-support/) contract).

### Acknowledgements
Tiny thanks [Aymane MAZGUITI](https://github.com/UncleJ4ck) and [Ange Primiterra](https://github.com/ange-primiterra) for their help identifying this vulnerability.

title: TinyMCE Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability through `mce:protected` comments
description:
### Impact
Stored XSS vulnerability via forged mce:protected comments. Allows attackers to bypass sanitization and inject scripts that execute when content is restored. Impacts users who utilize the protect option.

### Patches
Patched by validating decoded mce:protected content against configured protect regex rules before restoring. Users should upgrade to the latest patched version.

### Workarounds
No official workaround available.

### Fix
To avoid this vulnerability:

Upgrade to TinyMCE 8.5.1 or higher.
Upgrade to TinyMCE 7.9.3 or higher.
Upgrade to TinyMCE 5.11.1 LTS or higher for TinyMCE 5.x (only available as part of commercial [long-term support](https://www.tiny.cloud/long-term-support/) contract).

### Acknowledgements
Tiny thanks [Ivan Babenko](https://github.com/he1d3n) for their help identifying this vulnerability.

title: TinyMCE Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability using through data-mce- prefixed src, href, style attributes
description:
### Impact
Stored XSS vulnerability via unsanitized data-mce-* attributes (data-mce-href, data-mce-src, data-mce-style). Allows attackers to inject malicious values that override safe attributes during serialization, bypassing validation.

### Patches
Patched by stripping unsafe data-mce-* attributes during parsing. Users should upgrade to the latest patched versions (5 LTS, 7.x, 8.x).

### Workarounds
No official workaround available.

### Fix
To avoid this vulnerability:

Upgrade to TinyMCE 8.5.1 or higher.
Upgrade to TinyMCE 7.9.3 or higher.
Upgrade to TinyMCE 5.11.1 LTS or higher for TinyMCE 5.x (only available as part of commercial [long-term support](https://www.tiny.cloud/long-term-support/) contract).

### Acknowledgements
Tiny thanks [Tadi Kadango](https://github.com/mtrill47) ([website](https://tadiwakadango.com/))  and [Ivan Babenko](https://github.com/he1d3n) for their help identifying this vulnerability.

title: TinyMCE Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability using sanitization bypass through nested SVGs
description:
### Impact
TinyMCE 6.8.x contains an XSS vulnerability caused by improper SVG namespace scope handling in the sanitizer. A crafted payload using nested <svg> elements can bypass attribute sanitization and execute arbitrary JavaScript.

### Patches
This issue affects TinyMCE 6.8.x-7.0.x. The vulnerability is fixed in TinyMCE 7.1.0 and later.

### Workarounds
No official workaround available.

### Acknowledgements
Tiny thanks [maple3142](https://github.com/maple3142) (<https://maple3142.net>) of DEVCORE for their help identifying this vulnerability.

### References
Fix introduced in TinyMCE 7.1.0 though a rewrite of code causing the vulnerability.

status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-11420 (Web Application).

modified: 2026/06/05

    - attack.initial_access

    category: network_connection
    product: generic

        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3

// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-11420 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T20:34:58Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)

| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-11420", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-50733 - Markdown Preview Enhanced Arbitrary Code Execution via WaveDrom
id: 6a43a09d-eee9-589f-ac63-bc0b6c9458b6
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-50733 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-50733
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/05
modified: 2026/06/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: low
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-50733 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T20:34:58Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-50733", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-50733 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1092636; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-05, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

id: 19903e3f-92cb-5122-9319-82be0472b620
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-45748 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com

author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/05
modified: 2026/06/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: low
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-45748 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T20:34:58Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-45748", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-45748 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3038304; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-05, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

title: NASA AMMOS Instrument Toolkit: Path traversal resulting in arbitrary file append (can be triggered over the network by unauthenticated attacker)
description:
## 1. Summary

The Binary Stream Capture (BSC) component exposes an unauthenticated HTTP API for dynamically creating packet capture “handlers.” Because the code blindly trusts path‑related form fields, a remote client can:

- **Bypass the configured log root** and direct BSC to log to **arbitrary filesystem paths** (path traversal / directory escape), and
- **Append attacker‑controlled data** to those files, using the privileges of the`ait-bsc` process.

There are two ways for a remote attacker to trigger this:
1. If the attacker has access to the network where `ait-bsc` is deployed (a reason for that could be that the ports are publicly accessible), the payloads can be directly sent to the server to trigger the arbitrary file append. This type of attack is demonstrated in `python_poc.py`.
2. Even if the attacker does not have direct access to the network because the software is running in a local network, it is possible to exploit this if a bad actor in that network opens an attacker

title: Critical Cisco Unified CM Bug Patched as Public Exploit Code Emerges
description: Cisco patched a critical Unified CM flaw with public PoC code that allows unauthenticated attackers to launch SSRF attacks remotely. sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-0826 How an Old Bug Can Feed AI-Powered Impersonation
id: e3972809-26c5-5809-bc60-7a608639908e
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-0826 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-0826
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/05
modified: 2026/06/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-0826 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T20:34:58Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-0826", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-0826 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2541784; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-05, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
